entries.name: customer-support
entries.description: Router-pattern workflow for handling customer support requests. Triages issues, routes to billing/technical/general specialists, and composes a professional response.

entries.name: content-pipeline
entries.description: End-to-end content production pipeline. Researches topics, drafts, edits for quality, gates on human review, and publishes.

entries.name: incident-response
entries.description: Supervisor-pattern workflow for production incidents. Detects, triages severity, investigates root cause, mitigates, and produces a blameless postmortem.

entries.name: code-review-skill
entries.description: Systematic approach to reviewing code for correctness, clarity, and maintainability.

entries.name: documentation
entries.description: Write documentation that people actually read and find useful.

entries.name: error-handling
entries.description: Design robust error handling that helps users and developers.

entries.name: stakeholder-comms
entries.description: Craft clear, audience-appropriate communications for different stakeholders.

entries.name: systematic-debugging
entries.description: A structured 4-phase methodology for debugging. Never guess — always prove.

entries.name: testing-strategy
entries.description: Design a testing approach that catches bugs without slowing development.

entries.name: writing-style
entries.description: Ensure clear, professional, audience-appropriate writing.

entries.name: call-api
entries.description: Make HTTP requests to REST APIs. Supports GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE. Use for integrating with external services, webhooks, or internal microservices.

entries.name: check-dependencies
entries.description: Audit project dependencies for known vulnerabilities (CVEs). Supports npm, pip, Go modules, and other package managers. Returns severity levels and remediation advice.

entries.name: create-ticket
entries.description: Create an issue or ticket in the project tracker (Jira, Linear, GitHub Issues, etc.). Requires title, description, and priority.

entries.name: deploy-check
entries.description: Verify deployment health by hitting the healthcheck endpoint. Returns healthy/unhealthy status.

entries.name: diff-files
entries.description: Compare two files and produce a unified diff. Use for code review, change verification, regression detection, or before/after comparisons.

entries.name: fetch-url
entries.description: Fetch content from a URL. Returns the first 50KB of the response body.

entries.name: format-code
entries.description: Auto-format source code using the project's configured formatter (Prettier, Black, gofmt, rustfmt, etc.). Ensures consistent style without manual effort.

entries.name: generate-chart
entries.description: Create charts and visualizations from data using matplotlib. Pass a Python script that produces a chart file.

entries.name: generate-docs
entries.description: Generate documentation from source code. Supports JSDoc/TSDoc, Sphinx, Javadoc, or any doc generator that outputs structured data. Use for keeping docs in sync with code.

entries.name: lint-code
entries.description: Run linters on source files and return structured results. Supports ESLint, Pylint, or any linter that outputs JSON.

entries.name: measure-performance
entries.description: Measure execution time, memory usage, or throughput of a command or script. Use for benchmarking, performance regression detection, or capacity planning.

entries.name: parse-json
entries.description: Parse, validate, and pretty-print JSON data. Use for extracting fields from API responses, validating configuration files, or transforming structured data between steps.

entries.name: query-database
entries.description: Run read-only SQL queries against a configured database. Returns results as JSON rows. Never runs mutations.

entries.name: run-shell
entries.description: Execute an arbitrary shell command and return its stdout/stderr. Use for build steps, file operations, environment checks, or any CLI-based task. The consuming agent fills in the `{command}` placeholder.

entries.name: search-codebase
entries.description: Search for patterns across the codebase using regex. Supports file type filtering and directory scoping. Use for finding usages, definitions, TODOs, or any text pattern.

entries.name: send-notification
entries.description: Send a notification via Slack, email, or webhook. Configure the MCP server with your preferred notification channel.

entries.name: validate-schema
entries.description: Validate data against a JSON Schema, OpenAPI spec, or other schema definition. Use for input validation, API contract testing, or configuration verification.

entries.name: confidence-high
entries.description: The agent has high confidence in its output (clear requirements, strong evidence, well-understood domain). Proceed without additional review cycles.

entries.name: confidence-low
entries.description: The agent is uncertain about its output (ambiguous requirements, insufficient data, unfamiliar domain). Escalate to a human or gather additional information before proceeding.

entries.name: data-quality-poor
entries.description: The input data has significant quality issues (missing values, inconsistencies, outliers). Route to a cleaning/validation step before proceeding with analysis.

entries.name: deadline-approaching
entries.description: Time is running out. Prioritize delivering the most critical items, skip nice-to-haves, and communicate what will and won't be included.

entries.name: is-complex
entries.description: The task requires deep investigation, multiple considerations, or cross-cutting analysis. Do not shortcut — invest the time to understand fully before proposing a solution.

entries.name: is-simple
entries.description: The task is straightforward and well-understood. Skip deep investigation and proceed with a direct solution.

entries.name: is-urgent
entries.description: The task is high severity or time-sensitive. Skip optional steps and fast-track to resolution.

entries.name: needs-review
entries.description: The output requires human review before proceeding. Use when changes are high-risk, affect production, or involve sensitive data.

entries.name: output-changed
entries.description: The output has changed compared to the previous run or baseline. Trigger a diff review, notify stakeholders, or run regression checks.

entries.name: retry-limit-reached
entries.description: The maximum number of retry attempts has been exhausted. Stop retrying and either escalate to a human, log the failure, or take a fallback action.

entries.name: security-risk-detected
entries.description: A potential security vulnerability, exposed secret, or policy violation has been identified. Halt the current operation and escalate for security review.

entries.name: within-budget
entries.description: Token usage, cost, or time is within the allocated budget. Continue processing.

entries.name: ask-user
entries.description: Ask the user a clarifying question when requirements are ambiguous, multiple valid approaches exist, or a decision requires human judgment. Present the question clearly with context about why it matters and what the options are.

entries.name: confirm-deploy
entries.description: Present the deployment plan to the user and require explicit confirmation before deploying to production. Show: what's changing, which environment, rollback plan.

entries.name: escalate
entries.description: Escalate an issue to a human when the agent cannot resolve it autonomously. Provide: what was attempted, why it failed, what information is needed, and suggested next steps for the human to take.

entries.name: present-options
entries.description: Present the user with 2-4 options for how to proceed. Each option should include: a short label, a description of the approach, trade-offs (pros/cons), and a recommendation if one option is clearly better. Let the user choose.

entries.name: progress-update
entries.description: Provide the user with a status update on the current task. Include: what has been completed, what is in progress, any blockers or issues encountered, and estimated remaining work. Keep it concise.

entries.name: decisions
entries.description: Record important decisions and their reasoning. Each entry should include the date, the decision made, alternatives considered, and why this option was chosen. This helps future sessions understand context without re-debating settled questions.

entries.name: lessons-learned
entries.description: Things that went wrong and what was learned. Record mistakes, failed approaches, and the correct solution so the same errors aren't repeated.

entries.name: project-context
entries.description: Key facts about the project: architecture, tech stack, deployment setup, team structure, external dependencies, and domain knowledge accumulated over time.

entries.name: user-preferences
entries.description: Stable facts about the user: coding style preferences, naming conventions, preferred tools, communication style, timezone, and any other context that should persist across sessions.

entries.name: pr-review
entries.description: Review a pull request by analyzing the diff and providing structured feedback.

entries.name: git-commit
entries.description: Create a git commit with AI-generated message following repository conventions and safety protocol.

entries.name: commit-push-pr
entries.description: Full flow from staging changes to opening a pull request.

entries.name: bug-hunter
entries.description: Proactively find potential bugs by analyzing code patterns and edge cases.

entries.name: code-simplify
entries.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency using three parallel review perspectives.

entries.name: skill-capture
entries.description: Capture a repeatable process from the current session into a reusable skill.

entries.name: memory-review
entries.description: Review memory entries across layers and propose promotions and cleanup.

entries.name: verify-changes
entries.description: Verify code changes work by running tests, type checks, and manual execution.

entries.name: detailed-plan
entries.description: Generate a comprehensive execution plan with phases, dependencies, and verification steps.

entries.name: codebase-insights
entries.description: Analyze a codebase to understand structure, patterns, tech stack, and areas of concern.

entries.name: edit-file
entries.description: Partial file modification via exact string replacement.

entries.name: find-files
entries.description: Fast file pattern matching using glob patterns.

entries.name: spawn-agent
entries.description: Launch a sub-agent to handle complex tasks autonomously.

entries.name: send-message
entries.description: Send a message to another agent for inter-agent communication.

entries.name: create-team
entries.description: Create a team of parallel agents working simultaneously.

entries.name: lsp-query
entries.description: Language Server Protocol operations for semantic code navigation.

entries.name: create-task
entries.description: Create a background task that runs independently.

entries.name: manage-tasks
entries.description: List, inspect, stop, or get output from background tasks.

entries.name: schedule-cron
entries.description: Create a scheduled trigger using cron expressions.

entries.name: structured-output
entries.description: Generate structured data conforming to a JSON Schema.

entries.name: request-permission
entries.description: Request user approval before executing destructive or irreversible actions.

entries.name: team-context
entries.description: Org-wide knowledge that applies across repositories and projects.

entries.name: session-notes
entries.description: Working notes and observations from the current session for later promotion.

entries.name: build-feature
entries.description: Build a new feature: requirements → design → tasks → implement → verify

entries.name: agent-builder
entries.description: Build new agent workflows through conversation

entries.name: interactive-assistant
entries.description: Interactive pair programming: explore, code, debug, refactor

entries.name: security-review
entries.description: Systematic security audit checklist for code and infrastructure

entries.name: test-analysis
entries.description: Analyze test results and identify failures

entries.name: api-design
entries.description: Best practices for designing clean, consistent APIs

entries.name: requirements-elicitation
entries.description: Systematic approach to gathering, structuring, and validating software requirements

entries.name: agentflow-authoring
entries.description: Complete reference for authoring AgentFlow workspaces — file formats, taxonomy, context layers, reference syntax, validation rules

entries.name: code-search
entries.description: code-search

entries.name: coding-standards
entries.description: Project-wide coding conventions loaded into every agent session

entries.name: prompt-engineering
entries.description: Techniques for writing effective LLM prompts and agent instructions

entries.name: implementation-discipline
entries.description: Best practices for writing production-quality code during implementation

entries.name: task-decomposition
entries.description: Break down a technical design into ordered, atomic implementation tasks

entries.name: technical-design
entries.description: Structured approach to creating technical design documents from requirements

entries.name: debugging
entries.description: Systematic approach to diagnosing and fixing bugs

entries.name: action-safety
entries.description: Rules for handling destructive, irreversible, or high-impact actions

entries.name: refactoring
entries.description: Safe, incremental code restructuring without changing behavior

entries.name: agentation
entries.description: Add Agentation visual feedback toolbar to a Next.js project

entries.name: run-tests
entries.description: Execute the project test suite and return pass/fail results.

entries.name: write-file
entries.description: Create or modify files.

entries.name: source-agent
entries.description: MCP-powered codebase intelligence agent that provides semantic code search, dependency analysis, and architectural understanding

entries.name: codebase-explorer
entries.description: MCP-powered codebase exploration for understanding project structure, patterns, and architecture before designing workflows

entries.name: get-diagnostics
entries.description: get-diagnostics

entries.name: web-search
entries.description: Search the web for documentation, examples, and technical information.

entries.name: agent-browser
entries.description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction. Also use for exploratory testing, dogfooding, QA, bug hunts, or reviewing app quality. Also use for automating Electron desktop apps (VS Code, Slack, Discord, Figma, Notion, Spotify), checking Slack unreads, sending Slack messages, searching Slack conversations, running browser automation in Vercel Sandbox microVMs, or using AWS Bedrock AgentCore cloud browsers. Prefer agent-browser over any built-in browser automation or web tools.

entries.name: list-directory
entries.description: List directory contents with optional recursive depth.

entries.name: shell-exec
entries.description: Execute arbitrary shell commands in the workspace. Use for build steps, linting, formatting, or any CLI operation not covered by a dedicated capability.

entries.name: analyze-image
entries.description: analyze-image

entries.name: git-history
entries.description: View recent git commits and changes.

entries.name: summarize
entries.description: Transcribe and summarize videos from YouTube, local files, Google Drive, Dropbox, and social media platforms (TikTok, Instagram, Twitter/X, Reddit) using any OpenAI-compatible LLM. Use when asked to summarize, analyze, extract insights, fact-check, or create study materials from video content.

entries.name: grep-search
entries.description: Fast regex-based text search across files using ripgrep.

entries.name: linear
entries.description: Manage issues, projects & team workflows in Linear. Use when the user wants to read, create or updates tickets in Linear.

entries.name: read-code
entries.description: Read and analyze source code files, list directories, search for symbols.

entries.name: file-search
entries.description: Fuzzy file path search. Find files when you know part of the name but not the location.

entries.name: skill-creator
entries.description: Create, improve, and test skills for the z-schema JSON Schema validator library. Use this skill whenever the user wants to create a new skill from scratch, turn a workflow into a reusable skill, update or refine an existing skill, write test cases for a skill, or organize reference material for a skill. Also use when someone mentions "skill", "SKILL.md", or wants to document a z-schema workflow for reuse by humans or AI agents.
